mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, invalidating the earnings drag mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative median 60-day returns in over 55 percent of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving factory utilization and capacity expansion, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative following INDPRO increases during recovery phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent indicates extreme monetary accommodation that fuels liquidity-driven equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline by more than 2 percent over 20 days in 70 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent, contradicting the liquidity premium effect.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates reflects labor market tightening, which raises wage inflation expectations and compresses equity margins, driving negative returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST levels over a 4-year rolling window, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 0.5 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105 indicates severe capacity underutilization, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 58 percent of months where INDPRO drops below 105, or the signal's Sharpe ratio remains below zero across all tested regimes.
